title: Chronic Cough and Hemoptysis - Tuberculosis
chief_complaint: Productive cough for 3 months with blood-streaked sputum, night sweats, weight loss of 8 kg, and low-grade fever. Originally from India, immigrated 1 year ago.

physical_exam.chest: Right upper lobe crackles, bronchial breath sounds

hidden_data.cxr: Right upper lobe cavitary lesion with surrounding infiltrates and volume loss

key_learning_points: Upper lobe cavitary lesion = TB until proven
RIPE therapy: 2 months RIPE + 4 months RI

patient_responses.cough: I've been coughing for months, sometimes with blood
patient_responses.weight: I lost weight without trying, about 8 kg
patient_responses.sweats: I wake up drenched in sweat every night
patient_responses.travel: I moved here from India last year

hints.imaging: Upper lobe cavity is classic for reactivation TB

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Reactivation TB with classic cavitary lesion.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is pulmonary TB. The upper lobe cavity + positive AFB are diagnostic.
feedback.attending_note: Active TB: Start RIPE (Rifampin, Isoniazid, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol) for 2 months, then RI for 4 months. Airborne isolation. Contact tracing for close contacts. Monitor LFTs monthly. Pyridoxine with INH.
